content.claim: Free primary-school choice in Hungary operates as an independent segregation mechanism from the optional gimnázium tracks, with schools permitted to hand-pick students, creating residential and socio-economic sorting that is analytically distinct from early academic selection.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Quantitative breakdown of how much segregation is attributable to free choice versus selective tracks versus residential factors is not provided in the sources
The interaction between these mechanisms is not empirically isolated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
